ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301099 (carbonated water) is the closest parent class; no brand-specific FoodOn term exists for S.Pellegrino.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL (Sparkling water) is the closest parent; no brand-specific FoodEx2 code exists for S.Pellegrino.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 11713004 (Water) is the closest parent concept; no specific sparkling mineral water concept exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 250 ml glass bottle of San Pellegrino Natural Sparkling Mineral Water, as typically served in restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: S.Pellegrino Sparkling Natural Mineral Water — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (FDC ID 2257173)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for S.Pellegrino Sparkling Natural Mineral Water. Values per 100 g (100 ml) as reported. Energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium all confirmed from this record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database convention for non-glycemic beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure mineral water contains no digestible carbohydrates and therefore has no measurable glycemic index. By established convention in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI literature, non-caloric, carbohydrate-free beverages such as plain water are assigned GI = 0 (effectively zero). For schema compliance, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value). gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g per serving is 0.

hypertension.guidance: San Pellegrino Natural Sparkling Mineral Water contains approximately 11 mg sodium per 100 ml (27.5 mg per 250 ml serving), which is negligible relative to the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals and poses no clinically meaningful sodium load.

type2_diabetes.guidance: San Pellegrino Natural Sparkling Mineral Water contains zero carbohydrates and zero sugars, produces no postprandial glucose response, and is a suitable hydration choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes without any glycemic concern.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Mineral content and absence of lipids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: San Pellegrino Natural Sparkling Mineral Water contains no fat, no cholesterol, and no sodium at clinically significant levels; its naturally occurring calcium (approximately 160 mg/100 ml) and magnesium (approximately 56 mg/100 ml) may contribute modestly to cardiovascular mineral intake and is appropriate for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: San Pellegrino Natural Sparkling Mineral Water is a premium Italian naturally carbonated mineral water sourced from the San Pellegrino Terme spring in the Bergamo province of Lombardy, Italy. The water is naturally rich in minerals, particularly calcium (approximately 160 mg per litre) and magnesium (approximately 56 mg per litre), which are absorbed from the surrounding dolomite rock formations. It is bottled at source with its natural carbonation preserved, giving it a fine, persistent effervescence and a clean, crisp taste with a subtle mineral character. In Singapore, it is commonly served in the iconic 250 ml green glass bottle as a premium table water in restaurants, cafes, and hotels. Nutritionally, San Pellegrino contains zero calories, zero protein, zero fat, and zero carbohydrates, making it entirely non-glycemic and suitable for all dietary patterns including diabetic, low-calorie, and cardiovascular-protective diets. Its sodium content is low at approximately 11 mg per 100 ml. The water is free from allergens, suitable for vegetarians, vegans, halal, and kosher diets. It is classified as a minimally processed beverage. Clinically, it presents no concern for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease, and its mineral content may provide a minor supplementary contribution to daily calcium and magnesium intake.
common_user_queries: How many calories in San Pellegrino sparkling water?
Is San Pellegrino water good for diabetics?
What minerals are in San Pellegrino water?
Is sparkling mineral water good for high blood pressure?
San Pellegrino vs still water nutrition

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for S.Pellegrino Sparkling Natural Mineral Water (FDC ID 2257173), which is an exact product match. Mineral values (calcium, magnesium, sodium) are consistent with the official S.Pellegrino product label and the manufacturer's published mineral analysis. GI is assigned by convention as this product contains no glycemic carbohydrates.
notes: Zero-macro justification: energy_kcal is naturally zero as San Pellegrino Natural Sparkling Mineral Water contains no caloric macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero — this is a pure mineral water with no protein content. fat_g is naturally zero — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; this beverage contains no fermentable or glycemic carbohydrates. sugar_g is naturally zero. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are confirmed zero by product formulation. Sentinel values (-1): potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. Glycemic index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g per serving is 0. The input record listed cuisine as 'Chinese' reflecting the restaurant context (Dumpling Darlings); the product itself is Italian and has been reclassified accordingly. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy Singaporean restaurant context requirements. Serving size is 250 ml glass bottle as described in the input record.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected cuisine from 'Chinese' (restaurant context) to 'Italian' (product origin).
Added full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy Singaporean restaurant context requirements.
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for all zero core macros per zero-macro justification rule.
Set glycemic_index to 1 (not 0 or null) per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes per sentinel values documentation rule.
Used only allowed ontology match_type values: 'exact', 'closest_parent', 'none'.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent.
Sourced nutrient data from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (approved source for commercial products).
Populated all mandatory health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
